body_txt: When a character who is alive is mistakenly believed to be dead. Quite often includes the character being shown his own death certificate. Often includes being declared Legally Dead , but isn't necessarily restricted to that. In the most hilarious cases, it may result in a case of Attending Your Own Funeral and hearing a Premature Eulogy . Usually said as a Stock Phrase , with people and characters saying at least the "exaggerated" part when lampshading the fact they were alive all along. Named after a statement that Mark Twain didn't actually say , kind of. The actual quote was from a letter he wrote to the New York Journal: Subtropes A favorite tactic of supernatural killers, who return in the sequels after being thought to be destroyed in the previous film . See also First Law of Resurrection , where the character was meant to be Killed Off for Real , but was brought back from the dead in sequels. A form of Dramatic Irony if the audience is aware he's alive while the characters aren't. Often goes hand in hand with Our Hero Is Dead . It can be caused where someone is thought to have die (or killed), and reported as such, but they Never Found the Body , often leading to Burying a Substitute and/or a Prematurely Marked Grave .
